title: ZimerfeldTree - BranchHierarchy
gitFlowOutOrganizing: Hierarchy outside GitFlow ({0}) — showing GitFlow organization.
violLocalMasterRoot: LOCAL '{0}' should be a root, but has parent '{1}'.
violLocalDevelopChild: LOCAL 'develop' should be a child of '{0}', it is under '{1}'.
violLocalFeature: LOCAL '{0}' should be a child of 'develop'.
violRemoteMasterRoot: REMOTE '{0}/master' should be a root, but has parent '{1}'.
violRemoteDevelopChild: REMOTE '{0}/develop' should be a child of '{1}/{2}', it is under '{3}'.
violRemoteFeature: REMOTE '{0}' should be a child of '{1}/develop'.
newBranchPrompt: Name of the new branch from '{0}':
protectedBranchMsg: The main/master/develop branches are protected. Enable "Developer Mode" to delete them.
confirmDeletePrompt: Do you really want to delete the selected items?
forceDeleteConfirm: The branch '{0}' is not fully merged. Force deletion?
shaNotCaptured: Original SHA not captured — could not restore
resetLocalBranch: Reset local branch with the name:
createCustomBranch: Create local branch with custom name:
aboutTreeBody:
Buttons:

  ↺ (Refresh)
    Reloads the branch tree by reading the current git repository.

  Organize as GitFlow / Restore real hierarchy
    Reorganizes the tree following the GitFlow hierarchy
    (main, develop, feature/*, release/*, hotfix/*, support/*).
    Or shows it according to the real state.

  GitFlow Initialize
    Initializes GitFlow in the current repository (git flow init).
    Visible only in debug mode.

  Pull / Pull (↓N)
    Pulls commits from the remote into the current branch (git pull).
    The number in parentheses indicates commits to receive.

  Push / Push (↑N)
    Sends local commits to the remote (git push).
    The number in parentheses indicates commits to send.

  Commit / Commit (N)
    Opens the GitExtensions commit dialog.
    The number in parentheses indicates files with pending changes.

  GitFlow
    Opens the GitFlow window for the current branch:
    Publish, Track, Update and Finish.

  Restore
    Opens the restore window: restore a file from a commit,
    cherry-pick and reset a branch.

Context menu (right-click on a branch):

  Checkout             — Checks out the selected branch.
  New branch from here… — Creates a new branch from this one.
  Merge                — git merge of the selected branch into the current one.
  Rebase               — git rebase of the current onto the selected one.
  Rename…              — Renames the branch.
  Delete…              — Deletes the branch.
  GitFlow…             — Opens the GitFlow window for this branch.

Checkboxes (window footer):

  Show Debug
    Toggles debug tooltips: hovering over any control in the
    window shows the control's TYPE and ID (Name).
    Useful for inspecting the interface.

  Developer Mode
    Allows marking the main and develop branches for deletion.
    By default these branches are protected and locked
    (they cannot be marked or deleted). When the mode is disabled,
    any marked main/develop is automatically unmarked.
